top_signals.title: Oil Jumps 3% on Renewed US-Iran Conflict
top_signals.summary: Oil prices surged more than 3% on Monday after renewed military strikes between the United States and Iran reignited concerns over energy shipments through the Strait of Hormuz.Brent crude futures LCOc1 were up $2.67, or 3.51%, to $78.68 at 0743 GMT, while U.S.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Global stocks fall as Asian memory chipmakers hammered
top_signals.summary: Oil jumps as US-Iran hostilities escalate and investors go into ‘risk-off’ mode
